Scenario.title: Frankenstein Campaign: Disruption of Transportation Systems via Phishing and Data Manipulation
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple A sophisticated phishing campaign targeting transportation sector employees leads to unauthorized network access, followed by PowerShell-based payload execution and critical infrastructure data manipulation to disrupt operations.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Operational disruption and potential financial extortion

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Adversaries deployed spear-phishing emails with malicious attachments targeting transportation sector employees. These emails exploited human psychology to trick recipients into enabling macro execution, granting attackers initial access to internal networks.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Attackers leveraged PowerShell to execute payloads post-initial access, exploiting unpatched systems (CVE-2020-36193) to deploy malware and establish persistence mechanisms within the network infrastructure.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Critical transportation control system data (e.g., traffic signal timing parameters) was altered through input validation weaknesses, causing operational disruptions consistent with the Frankenstein campaign's sabotage objectives.

Scenario.summary: The Frankenstein campaign exploited human factors and software vulnerabilities to disrupt transportation systems, demonstrating the convergence of social engineering and technical exploitation in modern cyberattacks.
